evals.prompt: I have a conventions-heavy note. Help me design a reusable skill: when it should activate, what kind it is, and what stays in SKILL.md versus references.
evals.expected_behavior: Acts as an authoring-style reference: defines activation and anti-triggers, chooses reference vs task kind, keeps SKILL.md concise with shallow support files, and avoids jumping into a full package scaffold unless asked.
assertions: Defines when to use and when not to use the skill with concrete trigger language
Distinguishes reference versus task skill layering
Keeps SKILL.md concise and pushes lookup detail into shallow references

evals.prompt: Review this draft skill frontmatter and description for undertriggering, forbidden keys, and GPL license requirements.
evals.expected_behavior: Checks name/directory match, trigger phrases in the description, required license, draft kind, and forbidden provenance or undocumented keys.
assertions: Mentions description trigger phrases rather than domain-only labels
Mentions required frontmatter fields such as name, description, or license
Flags forbidden or provenance keys if present

evals.prompt: Build me a complete new skill package with SKILL.md, references, evals, agents/openai.yaml, and validation commands.
evals.expected_behavior: Recognizes this as end-to-end package building and routes to skill-creator rather than only applying authoring style guidance.
assertions: Does not treat pure style guidance as a full package scaffold workflow
Routes toward skill-creator or an end-to-end skill builder for packaging and evals
